title: Module 1
content:
Introduction
to
formal
language
theory–
Alphabets,
Strings,
Concatenation of strings,
Languages. Regular Languages - Deterministic Finite State Automata
(DFA)
(Proof
of
correctness
of
construction
not
required),
Nondeterministic Finite State Automata (NFA), Equivalence of DFA and
NFA, Regular Grammar (RG), Equivalence of RGs and DFA.

title: Module 2
content:
Regular Languages -Regular Expression (RE), Equivalence of REs and
DFA, Homomorphisms, Necessary conditions for regular languages,
Closure Properties of Regular Languages, DFA state minimization (No
proof required).Context Free Grammar (CFG)- CFG representation of
Context Free Languages (proof of correctness is required), derivation trees
and ambiguity, Normal forms for CFGs

title: Module 3
content:
Context-Free Languages -Nondeterministic Pushdown Automata (PDA),
Deterministic Pushdown Automata (DPDA), Equivalence of PDAs and
CFGs (Proof not required), Pumping Lemma for Context-Free
Languages
(Proof not required), Closure Properties of Context Free Languages

title: Module 4
content:
Context Sensitive Languages - Context Sensitive Grammar (CSG), Linear
Bounded Automata.
Turing Machines - Standard Turing Machine, Robustness of Turing
Machine, Universal Turing Machine, Halting Problem, Recursive and
Recursively
Enumerable
Languages.
Chomsky
classification
of
formal languages
